str:translations.str:about_feeds: This is a web feed, also known as an Atom feed. Subscribe by copying the URL from the address bar into your newsreader. Visit About Feeds to learn more and get started. It's free.

feed.title: ~/nbaig-dev - learning-opportunities
feed.subtitle: I am a backend web developer with 14+ years of experience. In this blog I will be posting my journey with the Rust Programming Language.

entry.title: Strategies for Healthy Remote Work in Tech
entry.summary: In recent years, remote work has become increasingly popular in various industries, particularly in the tech sector. While working from home offers numerous benefits such as flexibility and reduced commuting time, it also presents unique challenges, including isolation, distractions at home, and blurred boundaries between work and personal life. In this blog post, we'll explore ten strategies for maintaining a healthy work-life balance while working remotely in the tech industry. From creating a dedicated workspace to practicing gratitude, these tips are designed to help remote workers stay productive, focused, and connected both personally and professionally. Whether you're new to remote work or a seasoned pro, these strategies will provide practical insights and actionable steps for promoting a healthy remote work lifestyle. By implementing these tips, you'll be able to enjoy the benefits of working from home while avoiding common pitfalls such as burnout, isolation, and distractions. We hope that this post inspires you to make positive changes in your remote work routine and encourages you to share these strategies with your colleagues and fellow tech professionals. Together, we can create a more productive, collaborative, and connected remote work community!

entry.title: Overcoming Imposter Syndrome in Coding
entry.summary: In today's digital age, coding has become an integral part of our daily lives, with countless people delving into the world of software development. However, for many beginners or even experienced coders, imposter syndrome can be a major obstacle in their journey. This psychological pattern, characterized by feelings of inadequacy and self-doubt, affects individuals from all walks of life, including software developers.

entry.title: How to Deal with the Pressure of Being a Tech Interviewer
entry.summary: In today's rapidly evolving technological landscape, being a tech interviewer has become an essential role in many organizations. However, dealing with the pressure that comes with this responsibility can be overwhelming, particularly for those who are new to the role. This 8000 character blog post provides ten practical tips on how to manage this pressure effectively. From understanding the role and responsibilities of a tech interviewer to learning from every interview, this post offers insights into strategies that can help alleviate some of the stress associated with being an interviewer. The article also emphasizes the importance of evaluating soft skills, practicing self-care techniques, and collaborating with other interviewers. By following these tips, readers will be better equipped to make informed decisions regarding who is best suited for a particular position while managing their stress levels effectively. As a result, this blog post serves as an essential resource for tech professionals and recruiters alike, helping them navigate the pressures of being a tech interviewer with confidence and clarity.

entry.title: How to Deal with the Pressure of Coding Tests
entry.summary: In the fast-paced and competitive world of software development, coding tests have become an integral part of the hiring process. However, these tests can also be incredibly stressful, leading to anxiety and pressure that can negatively impact performance. In this blog post, we will discuss ten strategies for dealing with the pressure of coding tests, including understanding the test format, practicing regularly, managing nerves, reading instructions carefully, optimizing solutions, collaborating (where allowed), focusing on problem-solving, practicing time management techniques, and learning from feedback. By following these strategies, developers can build confidence in their coding abilities, perform at their best during coding tests, and continue to grow their skills over time. We hope this post helps you prepare for your next coding test with ease and confidence.

entry.title: How to Handle Criticism in the Tech Industry
entry.summary: In today's tech industry, where innovation and progress are constantly pushing the boundaries, criticism is an inevitable part of professional life for any tech professional. Whether it's from customers, stakeholders, or fellow techies, feedback can be both constructive and destructive. While some criticisms are easy to accept and learn from, others can be downright demoralizing. So, let's start our journey towards becoming better tech professionals!

entry.title: How to Handle Failure and Frustration in Coding
entry.summary: In the world of coding, failure and frustration are unavoidable realities that every developer encounters at some point. Whether it's buggy code, incorrect syntax, or system constraints, these setbacks can be discouraging and demoralizing. In this blog post, we explore ten strategies for handling failure and frustration in coding, providing insights and tips to help developers navigate these obstacles. From reframing your mindset and breaking tasks down to collaborating with others and learning new skills, our guide offers practical advice on how to approach these challenges with confidence and resilience. Whether you're a seasoned pro or just starting out in the world of coding, this post has something for everyone. So sit back, relax, and let's dive into some tips for managing failure and frustration in coding!
